children.name: Ciconia
children.description: Large soaring birds of open farmland and wetlands. The White Stork nested on Swedish farmsteads for centuries before disappearing in the 20th century due to drainage of wet meadows; an active reintroduction programme centred on Skåne has re-established a breeding population. The Black Stork is a shy forest bird, a rare but increasing breeder.

children.name: Ciconia ciconia
children.description: The white stork once nested on chimneys across southern Sweden before vanishing with the draining of wet meadows; a Skåne reintroduction project has restored a small breeding population. It feeds on frogs, insects, and small mammals in open grassland.

children.name: Ciconia nigra
children.description: A shy, glossy-black forest stork that nests in tall trees near undisturbed wetlands, in contrast to its conspicuous white relative. A rare but slowly increasing breeder and migrant in Sweden.
